Though meta-analyses may quantify the impact of such decisions, they do not resolve whether discrepant findings reflect mere design artefacts or genuine challenges to the theory [@meehl_why_1990]. By contrast, a formally specified theory predicts what discrepancies are **implied by the theory itself**. As an illustration, we use our JD-R model to predict the results of a cross-lagged panel model, a widely used analysis in the JD-R literature [@upadyaya_job_2016, @hakanen_job_2008, @sorjonen_questionable_2024]. We assess the cross-lagged relationship between job demands and work engagement with **lavaan** [@rosseel_lavaan_2012] on an ensemble dataset (*n = 10,000*), sampled at day 20 (wave~1) and three months later at day 110 (wave~2). 



```{r include = FALSE}
# First, install and load the required packages for data manipulation and structural equation modelling:
if (!requireNamespace("lavaan")) {
  install.packages("lavaan")
}
if (!requireNamespace("dplyr")) {
  install.packages("dplyr")
}
if (!requireNamespace("tidyr")) {
  install.packages("tidyr")
}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lavaan)
```


```{r include = FALSE}
# Next, we add random variability not only to the initial conditions of the stocks, but also to the model's constants. To avoid manually updating each constant, we can extract all constants into a dataframe, modify their equations, and then update the model with the modified dataframe.

# Random initial conditions
sfm <- update(sfm0, c(demands, resources, engagement, energy), eqn = runif(1, 0.01, 2))

# View constant values
df <- as.data.frame(sfm0, type = "constant", properties = "eqn")

# Update equation to draw from a uniform distribution
create_runif <- function(x, factor = 0.5) {
  paste0("runif(1, ", max(c(0, x - x * factor)), ", ", x + x * factor, ")")
}

# All constants can be updated at once by passing a dataframe to `update()`,
# which is more efficient than updating each constant separately.
df[["eqn"]] <- sapply(df$eqn, function(x) {
  if (grepl("^[0-9.]+$", x)) {
    create_runif(as.numeric(x))
  } else {
    x
  }
})

# Update model with modified constants
sfm <- update(sfm, df = df)
```


```{r include= FALSE}
# This modified model can now be used for the ensemble simulation, which will yield a dataset with variability in both initial conditions and parameters.

# Simulation study
sfm <- sim_settings(sfm,
  save_at = 1,
  # Modify stop to a round value so the last value is included
  stop = round(365 / 2)
)
n <- 10000
vars <- c("energy", "demands", "engagement", "resources", "performance")

sims <- ensemble(sfm, n = n, save_sims = TRUE)
plot(sims, vars = vars, which = "summary")
# plot(sims, vars = vars, which = "sims", i = 1:100)
```


```{r include = FALSE}
# Run cross-lagged panel analysis between energy and demands for different lags
# Preparing the data for lavaan involves reshaping the dataset into a wide format, where each variable at each wave is represented as a separate column. We then fit a cross-lagged panel model, which includes autoregressive paths (e.g., engagement at wave 1 predicting engagement at wave 2) and cross-lagged paths (e.g., engagement at wave 1 predicting demands at wave 2). The model also includes concurrent covariances between the variables at each wave.

wave1 <- 20
x1 <- "engagement"
x2 <- "demands"
lags <- seq(10, as.numeric(sfm$sim_settings$stop) - wave1, by = 1)

df <- as.data.frame(sims, direction = "wide", which = "sims")
dfX <- df[, c("i", "time", x1)]
dfD <- df[, c("i", "time", x2)]
dfX_wave1 <- dfX[dfX$time == wave1, setdiff(names(dfX), "time")]
colnames(dfX_wave1) <- gsub(x1, paste0(x1, "_wave1"), colnames(dfX_wave1))
dfD_wave1 <- dfD[dfD$time == wave1, setdiff(names(dfD), "time")]
colnames(dfD_wave1) <- gsub(x2, paste0(x2, "_wave1"), colnames(dfD_wave1))

build_wave_data <- function(dfX, dfD, wave1, lag, x1, x2) {
  dfX_wave2 <- dfX[dfX$time == (wave1 + lag), setdiff(names(dfX), "time")]
  colnames(dfX_wave2) <- gsub(x1, paste0(x1, "_wave2"), colnames(dfX_wave2))
  dfD_wave2 <- dfD[dfD$time == (wave1 + lag), setdiff(names(dfD), "time")]
  colnames(dfD_wave2) <- gsub(x2, paste0(x2, "_wave2"), colnames(dfD_wave2))
  dfX_wave1 |>
    dplyr::left_join(dfX_wave2, by = "i") |>
    dplyr::left_join(dfD_wave1, by = "i") |>
    dplyr::left_join(dfD_wave2, by = "i")
}

model <- sprintf(" # wave 1 -> wave 2
  %s_wave2  ~ 1 + %s_wave1 + %s_wave1
  %s_wave2  ~ 1 + %s_wave1 + %s_wave1

  # concurrent covariances
  %s_wave1  ~~ %s_wave1
  %s_wave2  ~~ %s_wave2
", x1, x1, x2, x2, x1, x2, x1, x2, x1, x2)
```


```{r include = FALSE}
# Choose one lag between waves for demonstration
lag <- chosen_lag <- 90

# Create wide format data for lavaan
data_wide <- build_wave_data(dfX, dfD, wave1, lag, x1, x2)

# Fit lavaan model
m1 <- lavaan::sem(model, data = data_wide, estimator = "MLR")

# Standardized parameter estimates (with standardized SEs/tests/CIs)
m1_est <- chosen_lag_est <- lavaan::standardizedSolution(
  m1,
  type = "std.all",
  se = TRUE,
  pvalue = TRUE,
  ci = TRUE
)
```


As shown above, our model implies that engagement has a strong positive effect on itself, whereas demands have little effect on future demands. Higher demands lead to lower future work engagement, but higher engagement increases future demands. Furthermore, our JD-R model allows us to assess how these effects depend on the time between waves. For instance, as shown below, though the autoregressive effect of job demands is initially negative, it flips in sign as the time between waves increases. If provided with only a verbal theory, there would be no principled basis for anticipating this lag dependence.



```{r include = FALSE}
# Storage
results <- vector("list", length(lags))

# Loop over lags
for (lag in lags) {
  data_wide <- build_wave_data(dfX, dfD, wave1, lag, x1, x2)

  # Fit lavaan model
  m1 <- lavaan::sem(model,
    data = data_wide, estimator = "MLR"
  )

  # Store standardized parameter estimates (including standardized SEs and CIs)
  out <- lavaan::standardizedSolution(m1,
    type = "std.all", se = TRUE,
    pvalue = TRUE, ci = TRUE
  )
  out <- out[out[["op"]] == "~", c(
    "lhs", "rhs",
    "est.std", "se", "ci.lower", "ci.upper"
  )]
  out[["lag"]] <- lag
  out[["est"]] <- out[["est.std"]]
  out[["est.std"]] <- NULL
  out <- out[, c("lhs", "rhs", "est", "se", "ci.lower", "ci.upper", "lag")]
  results[[as.character(lag)]] <- out
}

# Plot
results_df <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, results))
results_df[["path"]] <- paste(results_df[["lhs"]], results_df[["rhs"]], sep = " ~ ")
results_df <- results_df[, c("path", "est", "se", "ci.lower", "ci.upper", "lag")]
rownames(results_df) <- NULL
```


```{r include = FALSE, eval = FALSE}
# Check results
# Sort paths for maximum lag
results_max_lag <- results_df[results_df$lag == max(results_df$lag), ]
results_max_lag[order(results_max_lag$est, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# Chosen lag
results_df[results_df$lag == chosen_lag, ]
```


```{r include = FALSE}
# Relabel dataframe
x1_title <- stringr::str_to_title(x1)
x2_title <- stringr::str_to_title(x2)
label_map <- c(
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", x2_title, x1_title),
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", x1_title, x2_title),
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", x2_title, x2_title),
  sprintf("%s (wave 2) ~ %s (wave 1)", x1_title, x1_title)
)

names(label_map) <- c(
  paste0(x2, "_wave2 ~ ", x1, "_wave1"),
  paste0(x1, "_wave2 ~ ", x2, "_wave1"),
  paste0(x2, "_wave2 ~ ", x2, "_wave1"),
  paste0(x1, "_wave2 ~ ", x1, "_wave1")
)

results_df$path <- as.factor(label_map[results_df$path])

colors <- c(
  COLORS[VARS == "engagement"],
  COLORS[VARS == "performance"],
  COLORS[VARS == "resources"],
  COLORS[VARS == "motivation_rate"]
)
names(colors) <- unname(label_map)

# Plot lag-dependent parameter estimates with confidence intervals
pl <- plotly::plot_ly(results_df,
  x = ~lag, y = ~est,
  color = ~path, split = ~path, legendgroup = ~path,
  colors = colors,
  type = "scatter", mode = "lines",
  line = list(),
  showlegend = TRUE
) |>
  plotly::add_ribbons(
    x = ~lag, ymin = ~ci.lower,
    ymax = ~ci.upper, color = ~path, split = ~path,
    line = list(color = "transparent"),
    showlegend = FALSE
  ) |>
  plotly::layout(
    showlegend = TRUE,
    title = "",
    xaxis = list(
      title = "Time between waves (days)",
      font = list(size = font_size)
    ),
    yaxis = list(
      title = "Parameter estimate (standardized)",
      font = list(size = font_size)
    ),
    font = annot_font
  ) |>
  rm_grid() |>
  plotly::layout(
    xaxis = list(tickvals = list(10, 50, 90, 130, 170)),
    font = annot_font,
    legend = utils::modifyList(annot_legend, list(x = 1.7, y = 0.6))
  )
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
print(pl)
```

```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  export_plot(pl,
    file.path(filepath_figs, "JDR_SEM_across_lags.pdf"),
    width = 7, height = 3
  )
}
```

## Session Information

```{r echo=TRUE}
sessionInfo()
```


## References





## Remove:


## Stock-and-Flow Models

### Single stock example

```{r}
sfm <- sdbuildR() |>
  meta(name = "Burnout") |>
  sim_settings(stop = 16, time_units = "weeks", only_stocks = FALSE)
```

```{r}
sfm <- sfm |>
  stock(energy, eqn = .3, label = "Energy")

sim <- simulate(sfm)
plot(sim)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  sfm_list <- list()
  sfm_list[["A"]] <- sfm
}
```



```{r}
print(sfm)
```


```{r}
sfm <- constant(sfm, work_rate, eqn = .5)
```

```{r}
sfm <- flow(sfm, work,
  eqn = work_rate,
  from = energy, label = "Depletion from work"
)

sim <- simulate(sfm)
plot(sim)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  sfm_list[["B"]] <- sfm
}
```


```{r}
sfm <- update(sfm, work, eqn = work_rate * energy)

sim <- simulate(sfm)
plot(sim)
```



```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  sfm_list[["C"]] <- sfm
}
```

```{r}
sfm <- constant(sfm, recovery_rate, eqn = .3, label = "Recovery Rate") |>
  flow(recovery, eqn = recovery_rate, to = energy, label = "Recovery")

sim <- simulate(sfm)
plot(sim)
```



```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  sfm_list[["D"]] <- sfm
}
```

What if the ability to recover is not static, but erodes over time?

```{r}
sfm <- change_type(sfm, recovery_rate, new_type = "stock") |>
  update(recovery_rate, eqn = 1)
```


```{r}
sfm <- flow(sfm, erosion,
  eqn = recovery_rate * work,
  from = recovery_rate, label = "Recovery Erosion"
)

sim <- simulate(sfm)
plot(sim)
```


Counterintuitively, as recovery capacity erodes, energy first increases. This is because recovery first still exceeds energy depletion from work. 

```{r}
sfm <- aux(sfm, net_change,
  eqn = recovery - work,
  label = "Net change in energy"
)

sim <- simulate(sfm)
plot(sim)
```


```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  sfm_list[["E"]] <- sfm
}
```



```{r include=FALSE}
if (recreate_figs) {
  # Create subplot figure
  pl_list <- list()
  max_letter <- max(names(sfm_list))
  VARS_example <- c("energy", "work", "recovery", "recovery_rate", "net_change")

  VARS_example <- c("energy", "work", "recovery", "net_change")

  # Recovery = recovery_rate so plot as one line
  sfm_list[["E"]] <- sfm_list[["E"]] |>
    update(recovery, label = "Recovery / recovery rate")

  COLORS_example <- COLORS[seq_along(VARS_example)]

  titles <- paste0(
    paste0(
      "<i>", LETTERS[seq_along(sfm_list)], ". ",
      c(
        "Static Stock",
        "Constant Outflow",
        "Stock-Dependent<br>Outflow",
        "Constant Inflow",
        "Erosion of Recovery Rate"
      ), "</i>"
    )
  )
  titles <- stats::setNames(titles, names(sfm_list))

  for (letter in names(sfm_list)) {
    sim <- simulate(sfm_list[[letter]],
      save_at = .01, only_stocks = FALSE
    )

    vars_in_sim <- unique(sim$df$variable)

    vars_ <- VARS_example[VARS_example %in% vars_in_sim] |> rev()
    colors_ <- COLORS_example[VARS_example %in% vars_in_sim] |> rev()
    colors_ <- adjustcolor(colors_, alpha.f = .9)

    pl_list[[letter]] <- plot(sim,
      vars = vars_,
      colors = colors_,
      main = "",
      showlegend = (letter == max_letter)
    ) |>
      rm_grid() |>
      plotly::layout(yaxis = list(
        nticks = 3
      ), xaxis = list(
        tickvals = list(0, 7.5, 15)
      ))
  }

  x1 <- 0.1
  x2 <- 0.37
  x3 <- .78
  y1 <- 1.06
  y2 <- 0.48
  # y3 <- 0.45

  annot_ <- utils::modifyList(annot, list(
    font = list(size = font_size_subplot + 4, family = font_family)
  ))

  pl <- plotly::subplot(
    plotly::subplot(pl_list[1:4],
      nrows = 2,
      shareX = TRUE,
      shareY = FALSE,
      titleY = FALSE,
      titleX = FALSE,
      margin = 0.075
    ), plotly::subplot(pl_list[5]),
    nrows = 1,
    margin = 0.03
  ) |>
    plotly::layout(
      yaxis4 = list(range = c(0, 1)),
      yaxis2 = list(range = c(0, .4)),
      font = annot_font,
      legend = utils::modifyList(annot_legend, list(
        y = -.16,
        font = list(size = font_size_legend)
      )),
      annotations = list(
        utils::modifyList(annot_xlab, list(x = 0.5, y = -0.075))
      )
    ) |>
    plotly::layout(annotations = list(
      utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x1, y = y1, text = titles[["A"]])),
      utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x2, y = y1, text = titles[["B"]])),
      utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x1, y = y2 - .005, text = titles[["C"]])),
      utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x2, y = y2 + .005, text = titles[["D"]])),
      utils::modifyList(annot_, list(x = x3, y = y1, text = titles[["E"]]))
    ))

  pl

  export_plot(pl, file.path(filepath_figs, "build_sfm.pdf"),
    width = 7, height = 4
  )
}
```


```{r}
if (recreate_figs) {
  # copy cat(viz_str) from plot.sdbuildR() with minlen=1.5
  {
    viz_str <- "

    digraph sfm {

      graph [layout = dot, rankdir = LR, center=true, outputorder='edgesfirst', pad=.1, nodesep= .15]

      # Rank groupings
      {rank=same; 'net_change1'; 'net_change'; 'invisnode1'; 'energy'; 'invisnode2'; 'recovery_rate'}

      # Invisible edge to fix order
      'net_change1' -> 'net_change' -> 'invisnode1' -> 'energy' -> 'invisnode2' -> 'recovery_rate' [style=invis]

      # Define stock nodes
      'recovery_rate' [id='recovery_rate',label='Recovery Rate',tooltip = 'eqn = 1',shape=box,style=filled,fillcolor='#09B0ACBF',fontsize=18,fontname='Times New Roman']

     # Define invisible node
      'invisnode1' [id='invisnode1',label='Invisible Node',shape=box,style=invis,fontsize=12,fontname='Times New Roman', width=.2, height=.1, fixedsize=true]
      'invisnode2' [id='invisnode2',label='Invisible Node',shape=box,style=invis,fontsize=12,fontname='Times New Roman', width=.2, height=.1, fixedsize=true]

      'energy' [id='energy',label='Energy',tooltip = 'eqn = 0.3',shape=box,style=filled,fillcolor='#09B0ACBF',fontsize=18,fontname='Times New Roman']

      # Define flow nodes (intermediate nodes for flows)
      'work' [id='work',label='Depletion from work', tooltip = 'eqn = work_rate * energy', shape = plaintext, fontsize=16, fontname='Times New Roman', width=0.6, height=0.3]
                'recovery' [id='recovery',label='Recovery', tooltip = 'eqn = recovery_rate', shape = plaintext, fontsize=16, fontname='Times New Roman', width=0.6, height=0.3]
                'erosion' [id='erosion',label='Erosion', tooltip = 'eqn = recovery_rate * work', shape = plaintext, fontsize=16, fontname='Times New Roman', width=0.6, height=0.3]

      # Define external cloud nodes
      'Cloud1' [label='', tooltip = 'Unspecified source',shape=doublecircle, fixedsize=true, width = .25, height = .25, orientation=15]
                'Cloud2' [label='', tooltip = 'Unspecified source',shape=doublecircle, fixedsize=true, width = .25, height = .25, orientation=15]
                'Cloud3' [label='', tooltip = 'Unspecified sink',shape=doublecircle, fixedsize=true, width = .25, height = .25, orientation=15]

      # Define flow edges (stock -> flow_node -> stock)
      'energy' -> 'work' [arrowhead='none', color='black:#CCC568:black', penwidth=1.1, minlen=1, splines=false]
      'work' -> 'Cloud1' [arrowhead='normal', color='black:#CCC568:black', arrowsize=1.5, penwidth=1.1, minlen=1, splines=ortho]
      'Cloud3' -> 'recovery' [arrowhead='none', color='black:#CCC568:black', penwidth=1.1, minlen=1, splines=false]
      'recovery' -> 'energy' [arrowhead='normal', color='black:#CCC568:black', arrowsize=1.5, penwidth=1.1, minlen=1, splines=ortho]
      'recovery_rate' -> 'erosion' [arrowhead='none', color='black:#CCC568:black', penwidth=1.1, minlen=1, splines=false]
      'erosion' -> 'Cloud2' [arrowhead='normal', color='black:#CCC568:black', arrowsize=1.5, penwidth=1.1, minlen=1, splines=ortho]

      # Define dependency edges
      'energy' -> 'work' [color='#999999', arrowsize=0.8, penwidth=1, splines=true, constraint=false, tailport='_']
      'recovery_rate' -> 'recovery' [color='#999999', arrowsize=0.8, penwidth=1, splines=true, constraint=false, tailport='_']
      'recovery_rate' -> 'erosion' [color='#999999', arrowsize=0.8, penwidth=1, splines=true, constraint=false, tailport='_']
      'work' -> 'erosion' [color='#999999', arrowsize=0.8, penwidth=1, splines=true, constraint=false, tailport='_']
      'recovery' -> 'net_change' [color='#999999', arrowsize=0.8, penwidth=1, splines=true, constraint=false, tailport='_']
      'work' -> 'net_change' [color='#999999', arrowsize=0.8, penwidth=1, splines=true, constraint=false, tailport='_']

      # Define auxiliary nodes
      # 'net_change' [id='net_change',xlabel='',label='',tooltip = 'eqn = recovery - work',shape=circle,fontsize=16,fontname='Times New Roman', width=0.15, height=0.15, fixedsize=true, style=filled, fillcolor='grey90']
      'net_change' [id='net_change',xlabel='',label='',tooltip = 'eqn = recovery - work',shape=circle,fontsize=16,fontname='Times New Roman', width=0.15, height=0.15, fixedsize=true, style=filled, fillcolor='grey90']
      'net_change1' [id='net_change1',label='Net change in energy',tooltip = 'eqn = recovery - work',shape=plaintext,fontsize=16,fontname='Times New Roman', width=0.6, height=0.2, fixedsize=true]

    }
    "


    pl <- DiagrammeR::grViz(viz_str)
    pl
  }

  export_plot(pl, file.path(filepath_figs, "sfm_burnout_simple.pdf"))
}
```
